hero_summary: Shawq Afza is a branch of the Ajam family, and its identity is defined through the upper treatment and the coloration it produces within an Ajam framework.
short_identity: An Ajam branch with a distinctive upper color inside the family.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic according to the chosen root
quick_facts.ghammaz: Its importance varies according to the practical melodic route
quick_facts.upper_jins: An upper region with a distinctive color
quick_facts.character: Bright, colored, and clearly shaped in the jawab
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by stabilizing the Ajam foundation, then opens the upper region

what_is_it: This branch is understood inside the Ajam family as a maqam that retains the essence of Ajam, but develops a special upper color that gives it an audible identity within the family.

lower.name: Jins Ajam
lower.description: The lower Ajam jins remains the basis that ties this branch to the Ajam family.

upper.name: Distinctively colored upper region
upper.starts_on: After the tonic has been established
upper.description: Its special identity appears through the characteristic coloring in the upper region.

jins_architecture.transition_note: The difference from basic Ajam appears in the upper color and in the way the jawab is built.

sayr.summary: It confirms the tonic first, then develops an upper route with a special color before returning to reaffirm its belonging to the Ajam family.
resting_tones: Resting points within the lower jins
Upper stations specific to this branch
motion_notes: Its difference from basic Ajam is determined through the upper region
One should listen to how the jawab is opened and how the return from it is handled

transpose_explainer: When this branch is transposed to a different root, the interval logic and the modal functions that give it its identity inside the Ajam family must be preserved.
historical_notes: This branch is best understood through its relation to the parent maqam and through the difference that appears in the upper region.
school_differences: Its explanation may differ from one school or reference to another.
common_confusions: Listing note names alone is not enough to understand this branch; its motion and resting points must also be heard.
listening_notes: Begin by comparing it with the basic Ajam maqam.
Listen for where the distinctive color appears in the upper region.
